heroHeadline: Iran Sports Minister: 'Maybe the Decision is Not to Go' — Participation Still Unresolved 51 Days Before Kickoff
heroSubtitle: Iran's Sports and Youth Minister Ahmad Donyamali said on April 21 that 'maybe the decision is not to go,' directly contradicting FIFA President Infantino's April 18 declaration that Iran is 'coming for sure.' The final decision rests with the Iranian government and Supreme National Security Council, contingent on verifiable US player safety guarantees — with the FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30 (9 days away) as the formal decision point. Separately, Kansas City's $15 round-trip World Cup shuttle offered a sharp contrast to NJ Transit's $150 fare that continues to generate national backlash, while Fox Sports deployed Alexi Lalas lookalikes through the Boston Marathon to promote Gillette Stadium's seven World Cup matches.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, CNBC, NBC Los Angeles, Inside World Football, CBC Sports, Johnson County Post, PR Newswire, Yahoo Sports
